description: Replace Firebase Storage with Azure Blob Storage for profile picture uploads — managed identity only, public blob URLs, no signed URLs

userStories.title: Bicep infrastructure for Azure Storage Account
userStories.description: As a DevOps engineer, I need Azure Storage Accounts provisioned via Bicep so that profile picture storage is managed as infrastructure-as-code.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `infra/modules/storage-account.bicep` creates a Storage Account with `Standard_LRS` SKU, `allowBlobPublicAccess: true`, and `kind: 'StorageV2'`
Module creates a blob service child resource with a container named `uploads` with `publicAccess: 'Blob'` (anonymous read for individual blobs)
Module accepts a `principalId` parameter and assigns the built-in 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' role (GUID `ba92f5b4-2d11-453d-a403-e96b0029c9fe`) to that principal on the storage account
Module outputs `name` (storage account name) and `blobEndpoint` (e.g. `https://{name}.blob.core.windows.net`)
Storage account name parameter follows `burnbuddy{env}sa` pattern (e.g., `burnbuddybetasa`, `burnbuddyprodsa`)
`infra/main.bicep` includes the new storage module, passes `apiApp.outputs.principalId` for RBAC, and passes the blob endpoint URL to the API container app module

`infra/modules/api-container-app.bicep` accepts a new `storageAccountUrl` string parameter (no default), removes the `firebase-storage-bucket` secret and its KV URL from the secrets array, removes the `FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET` env entry, and adds a new env entry `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_URL` with `value: storageAccountUrl`

userStories.title: Install Azure SDK and rewrite storage library
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the storage abstraction layer to use Azure Blob Storage with managed identity so that route handlers can upload, delete, and reference blobs.
acceptanceCriteria: `@azure/storage-blob` and `@azure/identity` are added to `services/api/package.json` dependencies via `yarn add`
`services/api/src/lib/storage.ts` is rewritten: imports `BlobServiceClient` from `@azure/storage-blob` and `DefaultAzureCredential` from `@azure/identity`
Exported function `getContainerClient(containerName: string = 'uploads')` returns a `ContainerClient` authenticated via `DefaultAzureCredential`, reading `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_URL` env var
Exported function `getBlobUrl(blobPath: string, containerName: string = 'uploads')` returns the public URL string: `${AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_URL}/${containerName}/${blobPath}`
Exported async function `checkStorageConnectivity()` calls `containerClient.exists()` on the `uploads` container, logs a warning if it doesn't exist, logs info if it does — does NOT block startup
No connection string or SAS token support anywhere in storage.ts — managed identity only via `DefaultAzureCredential`

`services/api/src/lib/firebase.ts`: remove the `storageBucket` variable and remove `storageBucket` from all four `admin.initializeApp()` option objects (emulator, credential, ADC, minimal). Do not change anything else in firebase.ts.
Typecheck passes (`cd services/api && npx tsc --noEmit`)
userStories.notes: The firebase.ts cleanup is included here because it's a small change tightly coupled with the storage rewrite. Run `yarn install` after adding packages.

userStories.title: Update upload, delete, and account-delete route handlers
userStories.description: As a user, I want profile picture upload/delete to work with Azure Blob Storage so my photos are stored reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: In `services/api/src/routes/users.ts`, replace `import { getStorageBucket } from '../lib/storage'` with `import { getContainerClient, getBlobUrl } from '../lib/storage'`

POST `/users/me/profile-picture` handler: replace `getStorageBucket()` + `bucket.file()` + `storageFile.save()` with `getContainerClient('uploads')` + `containerClient.getBlockBlobClient('profile-pictures/{uid}/avatar.webp')` + `blockBlobClient.upload(optimizedBuffer, optimizedBuffer.length, { blobHTTPHeaders: { blobContentType: 'image/webp', blobCacheControl: 'public, max-age=86400' } })`
POST handler: replace `storageFile.getSignedUrl()` with `getBlobUrl('profile-pictures/{uid}/avatar.webp')` and append `?v=${Date.now()}` for cache busting
POST handler: the `profilePictureUrl` saved to Firestore includes the `?v=` timestamp param
POST handler: on storage upload failure, still returns 503 with `{ error: 'Storage service unavailable. Please try again.' }`

DELETE `/users/me/profile-picture` handler: replace `getStorageBucket()` + `bucket.file()` + `storageFile.delete()` with `getContainerClient('uploads').getBlockBlobClient('profile-pictures/{uid}/avatar.webp').deleteIfExists()`. Remove the try/catch that checks for code 404 (deleteIfExists handles this natively).

DELETE `/users/me` (account delete) handler: same pattern — replace Firebase storage delete with `getContainerClient('uploads').getBlockBlobClient('profile-pictures/{uid}/avatar.webp').deleteIfExists()`. Keep the try/catch that logs non-404 errors.
Remove any remaining imports of `admin` from `'../lib/firebase'` that were only used for `admin.storage()` (keep imports used for `admin.firestore`)
Existing sharp image processing (rotate, resize 256×256, WebP conversion) is unchanged
Typecheck passes (`cd services/api && npx tsc --noEmit`)
userStories.notes: All three handlers are in the same file (users.ts). The upload is the most complex change; the two delete handlers are mechanical replacements.

userStories.title: Update diagnostics endpoint for Azure Blob Storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the diagnostics endpoint to report Azure Blob Storage health instead of Firebase Storage.
acceptanceCriteria: In `services/api/src/routes/diagnostics.ts`, replace `import { getStorageBucket } from '../lib/storage'` with `import { getContainerClient } from '../lib/storage'`
Replace the storage health check: use `getContainerClient('uploads')` and call `containerClient.exists()` to check if the container exists
Response `storage` field includes: `storageAccountUrl` (from `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_URL` env var), `containerName` ('uploads'), `containerExists` (boolean result of exists check)
Remove `credentialsPresent` and `bucketName` fields from the response (these were Firebase-specific)
On error, `result.storage = { error: 'Failed to check container' }` — graceful degradation
Typecheck passes (`cd services/api && npx tsc --noEmit`)

userStories.title: Update profile picture upload and delete tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the upload and delete profile picture tests to mock Azure Blob Storage instead of Firebase Storage.
acceptanceCriteria: In `services/api/src/routes/users-profile-picture.test.ts`: replace all Firebase Storage mocks (`mockStorageSave`, `mockGetSignedUrl`, `mockStorageFile`, `mockStorageBucket`) with Azure Blob Storage mocks (`mockUpload`, `mockGetBlockBlobClient`, `mockGetContainerClient`, `mockGetBlobUrl`)
Mock `../lib/storage` to export `getContainerClient` (returns object with `getBlockBlobClient` method) and `getBlobUrl` (returns a test URL string)
Upload test verifies `mockGetBlockBlobClient` is called with `profile-pictures/{uid}/avatar.webp`
Upload test verifies `mockUpload` is called with the optimized buffer, its byte length, and blob HTTP headers (contentType + cacheControl)
Upload test verifies the returned `profilePictureUrl` contains `?v=` cache-busting param
Upload test verifies 503 response when `mockUpload` rejects (storage failure)
In `services/api/src/routes/users-delete-profile-picture.test.ts`: replace Firebase Storage mocks with Azure mocks — `mockDeleteIfExists` replaces `mockStorageDelete`
Delete test verifies `mockGetBlockBlobClient` called with correct path and `mockDeleteIfExists` called once
Delete test idempotency: `deleteIfExists` does not throw on missing blobs (no 404 error simulation needed)
All tests pass (`cd services/api && yarn test -- users-profile-picture && yarn test -- users-delete-profile-picture`)
userStories.notes: These two test files (362 + 172 lines) both test the users route handlers for profile pictures. Use vi.hoisted() + vi.mock() pattern consistent with existing tests.

userStories.title: Update diagnostics and account delete tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the diagnostics and account deletion tests to mock Azure Blob Storage instead of Firebase Storage.
acceptanceCriteria: In `services/api/src/routes/diagnostics.test.ts`: replace `mockBucketExists` and `mockGetStorageBucket` with Azure mocks — `mockContainerExists` and `mockGetContainerClient`
Mock `../lib/storage` to export `getContainerClient` returning an object with `exists` method (returns `mockContainerExists`)
Diagnostics test verifies response includes `storageAccountUrl`, `containerName`, and `containerExists` fields
Diagnostics test verifies error handling when `exists()` throws

In `services/api/src/routes/delete-account.test.ts`: replace Firebase Storage mocks with Azure mocks — `mockDeleteIfExists` replaces `mockStorageDelete`, `mockGetContainerClient` replaces `mockStorageBucket`
Account delete test verifies blob client is created with `profile-pictures/{uid}/avatar.webp` and `deleteIfExists` is called
Account delete test verifies storage errors are logged but do not fail the account deletion
All tests pass (`cd services/api && yarn test -- diagnostics && yarn test -- delete-account`)
userStories.notes: These two test files (186 + 443 lines) both indirectly touch storage. The delete-account test has many non-storage tests — only the storage mock setup and storage-related assertions need changes.

userStories.title: Update deploy workflow for Azure Storage
userStories.description: As a DevOps engineer, I need the deploy workflow to set Azure Storage env vars instead of Firebase Storage so deployments configure the correct storage endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: In `.github/workflows/deploy-api.yml` beta deploy job: remove the `FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET` fetch from Key Vault (the `az keyvault secret show --name firebase-storage-bucket` block and its `STORAGE_BUCKET` output)

In beta deploy job: replace `FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET=${{ steps.firebase.outputs.STORAGE_BUCKET }}` with `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_URL=https://burnbuddybetasa.blob.core.windows.net` in the `az containerapp update --set-env-vars` command
In `.github/workflows/deploy-api.yml` prod deploy job: same removal of `firebase-storage-bucket` fetch and `STORAGE_BUCKET` output
In prod deploy job: replace `FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET` with `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_URL=https://burnbuddyprodsa.blob.core.windows.net`
No other changes to the workflow file — Firebase project ID fetch and service account JSON secret remain unchanged
userStories.notes: Storage account URLs are hardcoded per environment since account names are stable and globally unique. This matches how other infra-derived values are handled in the workflow.
